description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Querétaro centred.
history: Querétaro has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms recall the legendary apparition of Saint James (Santiago) at the founding of Querétaro in 1531 and the Christianisation of the region, together with references to the colonial city and its place within the Mexican federation.
